---
title: Integrate the MB multiome data with fastMNN
date: 2025-02-06
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To integrated the Taylor lab MB tumour samples with fastMNN.


## Files and directories

### Code

The code can be found in `<tumour multiome software directory>/integrate_fastmnn.R` and all code was run using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment.

All input data are loaded from `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/MB_multiome`. Only the samples found in the JSON file (from Gabrielle Persad) were loaded and integrated.

Note that only the scRNA-seq data were used to perform the integration; the scATAC-seq data were not used for this process and removed from the final Seurat object.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  load["load samples"] --> tree{"integrate\nor merge?"}
  tree --> integ["if integrating"]
  integ --> integ_function["`SeuratWrappers::RunFastMNN`"]
  integ_function --> umap["`RunUMAP`"]
  tree --> merge["if merging"]
  merge --> merge_function["`merge`"]
  merge_function --> var_feat["get variable features with\n`SelectIntegrationFeatures`"]
  var_feat --> residual["recalculate residuals from\nSCTransform with `GetResidual`"]
  residual --> pca["`RunPCA`"]
  pca --> umap
  umap --> cluster["`FindClusters`"]
  cluster --> plotting["make plots"]
```

### Output

The output can be found in `<tumour multiome results directory>/20240206/`.

- `fastmnn` contains the results from integrating the MB tumour samples with fastMNN
- `merged` contains the results from merging the MB tumour samples without fastMNN (i.e., just concatenating the samples)


## Results

::: {#fig-umaps layout-ncol=1}

![](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/MB_multiome/20250206/fastmnn/umaps.png)

![](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/MB_multiome/20250206/merged/umaps.png)

UMAP plots after MB tumour samples were integrated with fastMNN (top) or merged (bottom). Cell are coloured by sample ID, MB subtype, or cluster. n = 60 samples, n = 317421 cells.

:::

Overall, the integrating the samples appears to work better than merging the datasets together. When integrating the samples with fastMNN, the MB tumour subtypes remain better separated with clearer boundaries compared with merging the samples together (@fig-umaps).
